title: Your Opportunities
subtitle: Fresh Istanbul and remote-friendly openings across medical tourism, international patient coordination, clinic operations, English-speaking sales, and customer success — screened against your CV so you can focus on the best fits first.

candidate.name: Nihal Kocaman
candidate.headline: International Sales Manager & Clinic Coordinator
candidate.experience: ~2.5 years in Istanbul dental tourism (Ekiz Dental, Swedish Clinic, MayClinik)

jobs.title: Uluslararası Hasta Koordinatörü (İleri İngilizce)
jobs.why: A very direct match: international patient coordination with advanced English. It uses your strongest CV points — English patient handling, clinic communication, CRM-style follow-up and sales coordination — though Kadıköy may mean an Asian-side commute.

jobs.title: Patient Coordinator
jobs.why: Patient coordinator is exactly your lane, and the medical/aesthetic clinic context is close to your dental-tourism background. Strong fit for your end-to-end international patient management and sales-to-aftercare experience.

jobs.title: International Patient Consultant
jobs.why: International patient consultant maps neatly onto your history with foreign patients, treatment explanations, pricing and follow-up. Prosthetics/medical tourism is broader than dental, but still close enough to make your background persuasive.

jobs.title: Sağlık Turizmi Uzmanı
jobs.why: A current health-tourism specialist role is a strong extension of your dental-tourism CV. It should let you use English, patient coordination, sales and clinic follow-up without being locked into only dental roles.

jobs.title: Sağlık Turizmi Uzmanı
jobs.why: Another clean medical-tourism specialist match. Your international patient coordination, clinic operations and sales reporting experience should translate well, especially if the clinic handles overseas patients.

jobs.title: Sağlık Turizmi Yöneticisi
jobs.why: This is a stronger seniority play: health-tourism manager rather than coordinator. Your clinic coordination, team leadership, reporting, recruitment and pricing/sales experience make it worth prioritising.

jobs.title: International Patient Coordinator
jobs.clinic: Dr. Bora Ok Aesthetic Surgery Clinic
jobs.why: The role title is excellent and the aesthetic-surgery setting is close to your health-tourism experience. It is marked good rather than strong mainly because Kadıköy may be a commute tradeoff compared with your European-side roles.

jobs.title: Patient Coordinator
jobs.why: This is back in your dental comfort zone and likely easy to explain in an application. It may be less of a step up than health-tourism manager/specialist roles, so it sits as a solid good match.

jobs.title: Sağlık Danışmanı
jobs.why: The listing is for an English health/sales consultant, which fits your sales, patient communication and clinic follow-up background. It may be more sales-heavy than coordination-heavy, but still very relevant.

jobs.title: Uluslararası Hasta İlişkileri Uzmanı
jobs.why: International patient relations is highly relevant to your CV and should use your patient-management strengths. The Asian-side location is the main tradeoff, so it is a good backup rather than top priority.

jobs.title: Customer Support Representative with English and Turkish
jobs.clinic: Conectys Turkey İletişim Hizmetleri LTD. ŞTİ.
jobs.why: This is useful as a hybrid, English-speaking fallback that uses customer communication and CRM habits. It is less specialised than medical tourism and may feel like a step down from clinic coordination.

jobs.title: Customer Representative
jobs.why: Remote work is the appeal here, and your English client-facing experience could transfer. It is outside healthcare and likely less senior than your clinic/sales background, so treat it as a lifestyle-flexibility backup.
